findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement Score
findings.description: The North Star metric for PersonaLens is the User Engagement Score, which reflects the overall engagement level of users with the platform. This metric is crucial as it directly correlates with user satisfaction and retention.
assumptions: User engagement is a key driver of retention and revenue.
recommendations: Monitor engagement score trends weekly to identify areas for improvement.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Content Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: Content CTR measures the percentage of users who click on recommended content. This is a primary KPI as it indicates the effectiveness of the recommendation engine.
assumptions: Higher CTR leads to increased user engagement and satisfaction.
recommendations: Aim for a 20% improvement in CTR within 60 days post-implementation of the recommendation engine.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: User Retention Rate (30 Days)
findings.description: User retention rate over 30 days is a guardrail metric that must not degrade, as it indicates the platform's ability to retain users after initial engagement.
assumptions: Maintaining a high retention rate is essential for long-term growth.
recommendations: Implement user feedback loops to improve retention strategies.

findings.title: Instrumentation Gap: User Behavior Tracking
findings.description: There is a gap in the current instrumentation for tracking detailed user behavior such as browsing history, click patterns, and scroll depth. This data is essential for enhancing the recommendation engine.
assumptions: Comprehensive user behavior data is necessary for effective personalization.

evidence.excerpt: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics.

recommendations: Develop and deploy the user behavior tracking pipeline as a priority.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: A/B Testing for Recommendation Engine
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing to compare the performance of the new recommendation engine against the current system. This will help validate the expected 20% improvement in content CTR.
assumptions: A/B testing will provide clear insights into the effectiveness of the new engine.

evidence.excerpt: Target: 20% improvement in content CTR within 60 days.

recommendations: Define statistical parameters for A/B testing, including sample size and significance level.

summary: The analysis identifies the User Engagement Score as the North Star metric, with Content CTR and User Retention Rate as primary KPIs and guardrail metrics, respectively. Gaps in user behavior tracking instrumentation were noted, along with a recommendation for A/B testing the new recommendation engine to validate its effectiveness.
